name: FDA warning limits commercial young-donor plasma claims
summary: FDA cautioned consumers that young-donor plasma infusions promoted for aging, memory loss, Alzheimer disease, and other conditions lack proven clinical benefit and carry plasma-product risks.
statement: FDA warned that young-donor plasma infusions promoted for aging-related uses have no proven clinical benefit and can carry infectious, allergic, respiratory, cardiovascular, circulatory-overload, and other plasma-product risks.
population_or_model: Consumers offered young-donor plasma infusions for aging or disease claims
time_horizon: Regulatory safety communication current as of February 19, 2019
caveats: The warning addresses promoted young-donor plasma infusions and does not directly evaluate every controlled clinical protocol.
It is regulatory safety context, not a clinical trial result.
It is especially relevant when interpreting preprint or commercial young-donor plasma claims.
